tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'optical stores Kyoto'
tasks.hook_line: 4 pairs of glasses in Japan could save $400+ vs US prices — where are the good shops in Kyoto?
tasks.reward: Locks in Day 1 shopping location so you hit the ground running in Kyoto
nuggets: Kyoto optical shops cluster in Kawaramachi district
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto, Japan. I'm planning to buy 4 pairs of glasses during my trip (March 25-27 in Kyoto). I need stores that accept international prescriptions, have good frame selection, and can do same-day or next-day service. I want a list of 3-5 recommended optical shops with addresses, Google Maps links, opening hours, and notes on their specialties or price ranges. Focus on shops in central Kyoto near Kawaramachi or main tourist areas.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Call insurance and ask: 'Does my vision plan cover glasses purchased internationally?'
tasks.hook_line: You're leaving tomorrow — need to confirm insurance coverage before committing to $800 in glasses
tasks.reward: Eliminates the risk of spending big on glasses that insurance won't reimburse
nuggets: Most US vision plans reimburse international purchases
Need itemized receipt in English from store
Prescription must be less than 2 years old
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out what I need to buy eyeglasses in Japan and get reimbursed by my US vision insurance. I have a US prescription from 6 months ago. I want to buy 4 pairs of glasses in Japan during my March 23 - April 7 trip. Questions: (1) What documentation do I need from my insurance before I go? (2) What do I need to get from the Japanese optical store for reimbursement? (3) Are there any restrictions on buying multiple pairs? (4) Do I need to get my prescription translated? Give me a clear checklist and any phone numbers I should call.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and create a new saved list 'Kyoto March 26-27'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples and you have 2.5 days — which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left on the route
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free entry, open 24hrs, best at dawn
Arashiyama bamboo grove crowds thin before 8am
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, nature, walking, good food, and interesting neighborhoods. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, entry fees, Google Maps links, and suggested walking routes. Mix major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) with less touristy spots. Include restaurant recommendations for lunch and dinner. We wake up early and like to beat crowds.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'things to do Takayama March' and open top 3 results
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is your mountain break between cities — alpine village vibes and spring hiking
tasks.reward: Secures your mid-trip mountain recharge before Tokyo intensity
nuggets: Old Town district walkable in 2-3 hours
Nearby Shirakawa-go village day trip via bus
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 2 days in Takayama, Japan (late March 2026). Traveling with Dana. We're coming from Kyoto and going to Tokyo after. We like nature, walking, mountains, local food, and authentic small-town vibes. Interested in: morning markets, old town, nearby day trips (Shirakawa-go?), and any good hikes or scenic areas. I want a rough 2-day plan with opening hours, transportation notes, and restaurant recommendations. Don't need minute-by-minute — just the highlights and how to move between them.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and create a new saved list 'Tokyo April 1-6'
tasks.hook_line: 5 days in Tokyo — where are the fountain pen shops, watch stores, and hidden neighborhoods?
tasks.reward: Completes the full trip itinerary so you can relax and pack
nuggets: Tsukiji Outer Market best 6-9am for breakfast
Nakano Broadway for vintage watches and anime
Marunouchi fountain pen district near Tokyo Station
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 5 days in Tokyo (April 1-6, 2026). Traveling with Dana. Interests: fountain pen shopping (Sailor/Pilot limited editions), watch browsing (vintage/affordable), food markets, quirky neighborhoods, temples/shrines, walking. Want a loose day-by-day structure with: (1) morning activity, (2) lunch area, (3) afternoon zone, (4) dinner recommendation. Include specific shops for fountain pens and watches with addresses and opening hours. Mix tourist spots with local areas. We wake early and walk a lot.

tasks.first_domino: Clone the Cerebro repo and open README.md
tasks.hook_line: Developer portal that's been parked for weeks — can you crack the local setup?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all future Cerebro work and gets you back into active dev
nuggets: Check for Docker Compose setup first
Common blockers: DB credentials, env variables, port conflicts
Look for Slack channels with setup troubleshooting
tasks.system_prompt: Help me get the Cerebro developer portal running locally. It's a work project that's been parked for a while. I need to: (1) clone the repo, (2) figure out the dependencies, (3) get the local environment running, (4) verify I can access the portal in my browser. Walk me through the typical setup steps for a developer portal (likely Node/React frontend, possibly Python/Go backend, probably Postgres). What should I check first? What are common blockers? Give me a troubleshooting checklist if things don't work.

recommended_slots.reason: Quick research task perfect for ramp-up energy, unblocks insurance validation, leaving tomorrow
Time-sensitive coordination before departure, low effort phone call fits ramp-up band
Kyoto planning deadline is today, medium effort research aligns with current energy
